examples.title: System Call Mechanism
examples.description: Demonstrates how a system call works internally in the OS
examples.code:
// Simplified System Call Flow
PROCEDURE system_call(syscall_number, parameters)
BEGIN
    // Save current state
    Push current CPU registers to stack
    Push return_address to stack
    
    // Switch to kernel mode
    Change CPU privilege level to kernel mode (mode bit = 0)
    
    // Locate and call appropriate system call handler
    syscall_table_index = syscall_number
    handler_address = syscall_table[syscall_table_index]
    Call handler_address with parameters
    
    // Return from system call
    Pop return address
    Restore CPU registers
    Change CPU privilege level to user mode (mode bit = 1)
    Return to calling application
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the internal mechanism of a system call: saving state, switching to kernel mode, finding the appropriate handler in the system call table, executing the service, and returning to user mode.

examples.title: System Call Example in C
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple system call wrapper in C for file operations
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates three common system calls: open() to create/open a file, read() to read data, and close() to close the file descriptor. These wrappers translate to kernel mode transitions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read where n is bytes read

examples.title: Python OS Module System Calls
examples.description: Shows how Python uses system calls through the os module
examples.code:
import os

# System call wrapper examples in Python

# getpid() - Get current process ID
pid = os.getpid()
print(f"Process ID: {pid}")

# fork() - Create a new process (Unix/Linux only)
# Returns 0 to child, child's PID to parent
try:
    child_pid = os.fork()
    if child_pid == 0:
        print("I am the child process")
        os._exit(0)
    else:
        print(f"Parent: created child with PID: {child_pid}")
except OSError:
    print("Fork not supported on this platform")

# getcwd() - Get current working directory
cwd = os.getcwd()
print(f"Current directory: {cwd}")

# mkdir() - Create directory (system call internally)
try:
    os.mkdir("test_dir", 0o755)
    print("Directory created")
    os.rmdir("test_dir")  # Remove directory
    print("Directory removed")
except OSError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")

# uname() - Get system information
system_info = os.uname()
print(f"System: {system_info.sysname}")
print(f"Node: {system_info.nodename}")
examples.explanation: Python's os module provides wrappers around system calls. These functions internally trigger the appropriate system call interface to request kernel services like process creation, file operations, and system information.
examples.timeComplexity: Varies by operation (O(1) for getpid, O(n) for read)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for most, O(n) for buffer operations

examples.title: Java Runtime System Calls
examples.description: Demonstrates how Java makes system calls through JVM
examples.explanation: Java uses the JVM as an intermediary for system calls. ProcessBuilder spawns OS processes, while internal JVM operations translate Java methods to appropriate system calls. The JVM handles the mode switching transparently.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for process execution where n is output size
